Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated blood and urine creatinine, significant proteinuria, and very high urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating severe renal dysfunction. Urine is abnormal in appearance, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, suggesting impaired renal concentrating ability and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Findings are consistent with acute or advanced chronic kidney disease; urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended.
